nodes.context: Albanian-American gang active in New York City metro area, involved in racketeering, extortion, gambling.
Italian-American Mafia family based in New York City.
Italian-American Mafia family based in New York City.
Greek-American organized crime group based in New York City.

edges.evidence_quote: Prosecutors say the Albanian gang was headed by Alex Rudaj and an Italian-American man named Nardino Colotti, who both had ties to the Gambino soldier Phil "Skinny" Loscalzo.
edges.context: Rudaj Organization leaders had ties to a Gambino soldier, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: Gambino leader Arnold Squitieri had had enough and wanted a talk with Rudaj. ... Squitieri told Rudaj to stop expanding their operations. Both sides had weapons, but Rudaj threatened to blow up the gas station with all of them in it.
edges.context: Rudaj Organization and Gambino family had a tense meeting with threats, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Rudaj led an attack in August 2001 on two Greek associates of the Lucchese crime family who ran a gambling racket inside a Greek social club on 30th St. in Queens.
edges.context: Rudaj Organization attacked Lucchese associates over gambling rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lucchese crime family , Gambino crime family , Velentzas crime family
edges.context: Infobox lists Velentzas family as a rival of Rudaj Organization.
